name: Festive Chicken --Meat--
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup orange juice concentrate OR mixture of orange & pineapple
1/2 teaspoon each crushed cloves and paprika
4 lbs broiler, skinned and cut into serving pieces
2 Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In an 8-ounce measure, combine 3 tablespoons honey.
2. Add the orange juice concentrate or mixture of orange and pineapple, crushed cloves, and paprika.
3. Fill to the 8-ounce mark with water and stir.
4. Pour into a wide bowl.
5. Rinse and dry the chicken pieces.
6. Prick all over with a sharp-pronged fork.
7. Add to the bowl, turning several times to coat. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
8. Drain the chicken, reserving the marinade. Arrange the pieces on a large flat plate.
9. Sprinkle with 1/4 cup matzo meal mix and press to adhere. Turn over and repeat until all is evenly coated. Chill in the freezer for 10 minutes.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large well-seasoned iron skillet.
12. In a bowl, combine onions, garlic, celery, and apples. Scatter 1/4 cup across the hot oiled skillet.
13. Cook for 30 seconds over medium-high heat.
14. Add half the chicken pieces and lightly brown on both sides (5 minutes), scooping up and turning with a sharp-edged spatula.
15. Transfer the cooked chicken and browned particles to a plate. Add another tablespoon of oil.
16. Repeat until all is browned.
17. Saute the remaining vegetable-apple mixture until lightly browned, scraping bottom of skillet to loosen particles.
18. Add the marinade around the edges of the skillet. Stir in 2 tablespoons parsley and bring to a boil, combining.
19. Return the chicken to the pan, spooning with the thick sauce. Cover and simmer on top of the stove for 3 minutes.
20. Baste the chicken.
21. Place in the center of the oven for 50 minutes, spooning with sauce once midway.
22. Remove from the oven. Sprinkle salt evenly over all.
23. In a cup, blend the remaining tablespoon honey with lemon juice and mustard. Pour over the chicken.
24. Re-cover and let stand for 5 minutes.
25. Drain the chicken of sauce and arrange on a large warmed serving plate.
26. Stir the sauce and reheat briefly. Spoon some over the chicken.
27. Cut each pineapple slice into thirds and arrange around the sides of the plate in a neat pattern.
28. Serve immediately, sprinkled with remaining parsley; serve extra sauce on the side.
Yield: 4 or 5 servings
